code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test04-OCT-2020Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, and elevated creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating significant renal and metabolic dysfunction. Findings may suggest acute or chronic kidney disease with possible tubular dysfunction and metabolic derangement. Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
